name: Metallurgical Engineering
description: Metallurgical Engineering deals with the study of metals, their extraction, processing, and application. It encompasses physical metallurgy, mechanical behavior, and materials characterization for diverse industrial applications.

subjects.name: Physical Metallurgy
subjects.description: Study of crystal structures, phase diagrams, and solidification behavior of metals and alloys. Covers atomic arrangements, diffusion mechanisms, and microstructure-property relationships.
learning_outcomes: Analyze crystal structures and defects in metallic materials
Interpret binary and ternary phase diagrams
Explain solidification mechanisms and microstructural evolution
Relate microstructure to mechanical properties
subjects.industry_relevance: Foundational knowledge used in steel plants, foundries, and alloy manufacturing industries worldwide.

subjects.name: Mechanical Metallurgy
subjects.description: Examines the mechanical behavior of metals under various loading conditions including elastic and plastic deformation. Covers fracture mechanics, fatigue, creep, and stress-strain relationships.
learning_outcomes: Apply stress-strain analysis to predict material failure
Evaluate fracture toughness and fatigue life of metallic components
Design against creep and high-temperature deformation
Select appropriate testing methods for mechanical characterization
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for aerospace, automotive, and structural engineering sectors where material failure prevention is paramount.

subjects.name: Extractive Metallurgy
subjects.description: Covers principles and processes for extracting metals from ores including pyrometallurgy, hydrometallurgy, and electrometallurgy. Includes thermodynamics of reduction, roasting, smelting, and refining operations.
learning_outcomes: Design extraction flowsheets for ferrous and non-ferrous metals
Apply thermodynamic principles to metal reduction processes
Evaluate environmental impacts of extraction processes
Optimize process parameters for maximum metal recovery
subjects.industry_relevance: Directly applicable to mining and mineral processing industries including steel, aluminum, copper, and rare earth extraction.

subjects.name: Phase Transformations
subjects.description: In-depth study of phase transformation kinetics including nucleation, growth, and diffusion-controlled transformations. Covers martensitic transformations, bainitic reactions, and precipitation hardening.
learning_outcomes: Predict phase transformation products using TTT and CCT diagrams
Analyze nucleation and growth kinetics in solid-state transformations
Design heat treatment cycles based on transformation behavior
Characterize transformed microstructures using microscopy techniques
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for heat treatment facilities, automotive component manufacturing, and special steel production.

subjects.name: Heat Treatment
subjects.description: Study of heat treatment processes including annealing, normalizing, quenching, tempering, and surface hardening. Focuses on process optimization, furnace design, and property enhancement of steels and non-ferrous alloys.
learning_outcomes: Design heat treatment cycles for desired microstructure and properties
Select appropriate quenching media and furnace types
Troubleshoot heat treatment defects like distortion and cracking
Apply surface hardening techniques for wear resistance
subjects.industry_relevance: Widely used in automotive, tool manufacturing, aerospace, and heavy machinery industries for property enhancement.

subjects.name: Materials Characterization
subjects.description: Covers analytical techniques for characterizing materials including optical microscopy, SEM, TEM, XRD, EDS, and thermal analysis. Emphasizes sample preparation, data interpretation, and microstructural analysis.
learning_outcomes: Operate optical and electron microscopes for microstructural analysis
Interpret X-ray diffraction patterns for phase identification
Apply thermal analysis techniques for material property evaluation
Select appropriate characterization method for specific material problems
subjects.industry_relevance: Indispensable in R&D labs, quality control departments, and failure analysis facilities across manufacturing sectors.

subjects.name: Corrosion Engineering
subjects.description: Study of corrosion mechanisms, forms of corrosion, and protection methods including coatings, inhibitors, and cathodic protection. Covers electrochemical principles and corrosion testing standards.
learning_outcomes: Identify corrosion mechanisms using electrochemical techniques
Design cathodic protection systems for pipelines and structures
Select appropriate coatings and inhibitors for corrosion prevention
Conduct corrosion rate measurements and material compatibility tests
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for oil and gas, marine, chemical processing, and infrastructure industries facing corrosion-related failures.

subjects.name: Welding Metallurgy
subjects.description: Examines metallurgical aspects of welding including heat-affected zone, weld solidification, and joint integrity. Covers welding processes, filler materials, and defect analysis in ferrous and non-ferrous alloys.
learning_outcomes: Analyze microstructural changes in weld heat-affected zones
Select appropriate welding parameters for different alloys
Diagnose and prevent common welding defects
Design weld procedures for structural integrity
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for shipbuilding, pressure vessel fabrication, pipeline construction, and structural steel industries.

subjects.name: Composite Materials
subjects.description: Study of metal matrix composites, ceramic matrix composites, and polymer matrix composites. Covers reinforcement types, fabrication methods, interface engineering, and mechanical properties.
learning_outcomes: Design composite architectures for specific property requirements
Select appropriate reinforcement-matrix combinations
Evaluate mechanical and thermal properties of composites
Apply composite manufacturing techniques including infiltration and layup
subjects.industry_relevance: Used extensively in aerospace, defense, automotive, and sporting goods industries for lightweight high-performance components.

subjects.name: Ceramics & Glasses
subjects.description: Covers structure, properties, and processing of advanced ceramics and glasses including refractories, bioceramics, and electronic ceramics. Topics include sintering, glass transition, and mechanical behavior of brittle materials.
learning_outcomes: Classify ceramic materials based on structure and bonding
Apply sintering theory to produce dense ceramic components
Analyze glass formation and crystallization behavior
Select ceramics for high-temperature and biomedical applications
subjects.industry_relevance: Important for refractory manufacturing, electronics, biomedical implants, and aerospace thermal protection systems.

subjects.name: Powder Metallurgy
subjects.description: Study of metal powder production, compaction, sintering, and post-processing operations. Covers powder characterization, tooling design, and applications of PM components in automotive and cutting tool industries.
learning_outcomes: Characterize metal powders for size, shape, and flow properties
Design compaction cycles and tooling for net-shape manufacturing
Optimize sintering parameters for densification and properties
Evaluate PM applications for automotive and aerospace components
subjects.industry_relevance: Key technology for high-volume production of precision components in automotive, power tools, and electrical industries.

subjects.name: Non-Destructive Testing
subjects.description: Covers NDT methods including ultrasonic testing, radiography, magnetic particle inspection, liquid penetrant testing, and eddy current testing. Emphasizes flaw detection, standards compliance, and certification procedures.
learning_outcomes: Apply ultrasonic testing principles for volumetric flaw detection
Interpret radiographic images for weld and casting inspection
Select appropriate NDT method based on material and defect type
Comply with ASME and ASTM standards for NDT certification
subjects.industry_relevance: Mandatory quality assurance practice in aerospace, oil and gas, power generation, and structural fabrication industries.

subjects.name: Metal Forming
subjects.description: Study of bulk and sheet metal forming processes including rolling, forging, extrusion, drawing, and stamping. Covers plasticity theory, formability analysis, process simulation, and die design.
learning_outcomes: Analyze metal flow and stress distribution in forming operations
Design rolling schedules and forging sequences for defect-free products
Apply finite element methods for forming process simulation
Optimize process parameters for improved formability
subjects.industry_relevance: Backbone of manufacturing industries including automotive body panels, structural sections, and aerospace components.

subjects.name: Foundry Technology
subjects.description: Covers casting processes, mold design, gating and riser systems, solidification modeling, and defect analysis. Includes sand casting, investment casting, die casting, and continuous casting techniques.
learning_outcomes: Design gating and riser systems for sound castings
Simulate solidification patterns to predict casting defects
Select appropriate casting process based on component geometry
Troubleshoot common casting defects including porosity and shrinkage
subjects.industry_relevance: Foundational for automotive casting, pump and valve manufacturing, and heavy machinery component production.

subjects.name: Alloy Design
subjects.description: Covers principles of alloy development including thermodynamics, phase stability, and property optimization. Explores advanced alloys such as superalloys, shape memory alloys, and high-entropy alloys.
learning_outcomes: Apply CALPHAD methodology for alloy composition design
Design high-temperature superalloys for turbine applications
Develop alloys with tailored magnetic and shape memory properties
Optimize composition-microstructure-property relationships
subjects.industry_relevance: Drives innovation in aerospace turbine materials, biomedical implants, and advanced energy systems.

subjects.name: Failure Analysis
subjects.description: Systematic approach to analyzing material failures including fracture, corrosion, wear, and fatigue failures. Covers forensic investigation techniques, fractography, and root cause analysis methodologies.
learning_outcomes: Conduct systematic failure investigations using fractography
Identify failure modes from fracture surface features
Prepare expert reports for litigation and insurance claims
Recommend preventive measures based on root cause analysis
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for liability assessment, insurance claims, and preventing catastrophic failures in industrial equipment.

subjects.name: Nanomaterials
subjects.description: Study of nanoscale materials including nanoparticles, nanotubes, nanowires, and thin films. Covers synthesis methods, characterization techniques, and size-dependent properties with engineering applications.
learning_outcomes: Synthesize nanomaterials using top-down and bottom-up approaches
Characterize nanostructures using electron microscopy and spectroscopy
Explain quantum confinement and surface effects in nanomaterials
Design nanomaterial-based solutions for energy and biomedical applications
subjects.industry_relevance: Transforming industries from electronics and energy storage to medicine and environmental remediation.

subjects.name: Computational Materials Science
subjects.description: Covers computational methods for materials modeling including DFT, molecular dynamics, phase-field simulations, and CALPHAD. Emphasizes practical application of simulation tools for materials design.
learning_outcomes: Apply density functional theory for electronic structure calculations
Perform molecular dynamics simulations for material property prediction
Use phase-field modeling for microstructural evolution studies
Integrate CALPHAD with process simulation for alloy development
subjects.industry_relevance: Increasingly used in materials R&D for accelerated alloy design and reducing experimental trial-and-error costs.
